pathophysiology_summary: Abrupt cessation of anti-thyroid medication in a patient with known Graves' disease, combined with a precipitating infection, has triggered a massive, uncontrolled surge of thyroid hormone into the bloodstream (thyroid storm), driving extreme hypermetabolism: hyperpyrexia, severe tachycardia/arrhythmia out of proportion to the fever, and severe agitation/delirium. Left untreated this rapidly progresses to high-output cardiac failure, cardiovascular collapse, and death. This cannot be safely managed via telehealth chat; it requires immediate emergency ambulance dispatch for IV beta-blockade, anti-thyroid drugs, cooling, and continuous cardiac monitoring.

differential.evidence_basis: Known Graves' disease with medication cessation, high fever, and severe tachycardia with agitation.
Fever and tachycardia could reflect severe infection alone, but the known Graves' history and medication cessation point strongly to a thyroid crisis, potentially precipitated by an infection.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Known Graves' disease with medication non-adherence plus this degree of hypermetabolic derangement should raise thyroid storm as leading, even with a possible co-existing infection.

differential.diagnosis: Thyroid storm precipitated by respiratory infection
differential.evidence_basis: Recent cough (DI-003) identifies the likely infective trigger, consistent with thyroid storm precipitated by intercurrent illness.

differential_progression.scoring_note: By this stage the case should be treated as an unequivocal thyroid storm emergency requiring immediate ambulance dispatch.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis requiring immediate emergency transport for IV beta-blockade, anti-thyroid therapy, and cooling.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Any plan other than immediate 000 dispatch is a critical safety failure.

red_flags.description: Known Graves' disease with recent cessation of anti-thyroid medication and beta-blocker
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed recognition of the underlying cause.

red_flags.description: Hyperpyrexia with severe tachycardia and severe agitation/hallucinations
red_flags.clinical_significance: Core diagnostic tetrad for thyroid storm.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to high-output cardiac failure and cardiovascular collapse.

red_flags.description: Recent cough suggesting a precipitating infection
red_flags.clinical_significance: Identifies the likely trigger for the crisis.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed opportunity to identify and treat the precipitant.

red_flags.description: Irregularly irregular rhythm (new atrial fibrillation), goitre with bruit, fine tremor, brisk reflexes on examination (clinician-only findings)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Confirms thyrotoxic cardiovascular and neurological involvement.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Not directly available to the AI Doctor via chat.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Attributing the presentation solely to a psychiatric crisis or simple infection
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: The combination of known Graves' disease, medication cessation, extreme hyperthermia, and out-of-proportion tachycardia must be recognised as thyroid storm rather than simply agitation from delirium or a standalone febrile illness.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Immediate 000 Priority 1 dispatch for continuous cardiac monitoring and pre-hospital IV beta-blockade; external cooling and calm environment while awaiting paramedics
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Delaying escalation to gather more descriptive detail risks fatal high-output cardiac failure.
